title: Andrew Ng's Machine Learning Collection
excerpts:
Section Title: Andrew Ng’s Machine Learning Collection
Content:
Explore
Andrew Ng’s Machine Learning Collection
Courses and specializations from leading organizations and universities, curated by Andrew Ng
Andrew Ng is founder of DeepLearning.AI, general partner at AI Fund, chairman and cofounder of Coursera, and an adjunct professor at Stanford University. As a pioneer both in machine learning and online education, Dr. Ng has changed countless lives through his work in AI, authoring or co-authoring over 100 research papers in machine learning, robotics, and related fields.
Join a **community** of **over 100 million learners** from around the world
Learn from more than **200 leading universities** and industry educators.
**70%** of all **learners** who have stated a career goal and completed a course report outcomes such as gaining confidence, improving work performance, or selecting a new career path.
All courses include:

title: From Zero to Hero: The Journey of Andrew Ng, Renowned ...
excerpts:
Section Title: From Zero to Hero: The Journey of Andrew Ng, Renowned AI Expert > Key Milestones and Achievements
Content:
**Founding Google Brain:** One of Ng’s most significant contributions to the AI community was co-founding Google Brain, a deep learning research project at Google. Launched in 2011, Google Brain aimed to harness the power of neural networks to improve Google’s products and services. Under Ng’s leadership, the project achieved remarkable success, including the development of algorithms that significantly enhanced image and speech recognition technologies.
**Stanford and Coursera:** Ng’s commitment to education led him to join the faculty at Stanford University, where he became a beloved professor and researcher. In 2012, he co-founded Coursera, an online learning platform, with the vision of making high-quality education accessible to everyone. Coursera quickly grew into one of the largest online education platforms, offering courses from top universities around the

title: Andrew Ng's latest letter: It's time to focus on parallel agents-Electronics Headlines-EEWORLD
excerpts:
Section Title: Andrew Ng's latest letter: It's time to focus on parallel agents > Parallel agents are the future
Content:
Finally, let's talk about something off topic.
In 2009, Andrew Ng systematically demonstrated the large-scale application of GPUs in deep learning for the first time in his classic paper "Large-Scale Deep Unsupervised Learning Using Graphics Processors".
This paper not only proves that GPU computing can significantly improve the training efficiency of convolutional neural networks, but also heralds the arrival of the era of deep learning.
The core of all this comes down to two words:
**parallel** .

title: Releasing a new "Agentic Reviewer" for research papers. ...
excerpts:
Section Title: Conversation
Content:
Andrew Ng
@AndrewYNg
Releasing a new " Agentic Reviewer " for research papers. I started coding this as a weekend project, and
@jyx_su
made it much better. I was inspired by a student who had a paper rejected 6 times over 3 years. Their feedback loop -- waiting ~6 months for feedback each time -- was painfully slow. We wanted to see if an agentic workflow can help researchers iterate faster. When we trained the system on ICLR 2025 reviews and measured Spearman correlation (higher is better) on the test set: - Correlation between two human reviewers: 0.41 - Correlation between AI and a human reviewer: 0.42 This suggests agentic reviewing is approaching human-level performance. The agent grounds its feedback by searching arXiv, so it works best in fields like AI where research is freely published there. It’s an experimental tool, but I hope it helps you with your research. Check it out here: [http:// paperreview.ai](https://t.co/n7ctnDilJJ)
12:01 pm ·
